purpose: Govern how Meta-Runtime-Memory binds, switches, and persists the active task context.
instruction: Each durable task must have a stable task_id and its own task directory.
ACTIVE_TASK.json is the single pointer to the current task.
Automatic binding must also persist session_id + turn_id attribution through ACTIVE_RUNTIME.json and TURN_AUDIT.json.
Binding a task creates the task directory when absent and updates the pointer immediately.
When the user switches tasks, update the pointer before continuing any task-specific work.
workflow: Derive or match the durable task identifier from the current session turn.
Create tasks/<task_id>/TASK_MEMORY.json and TURN_DELTA.json when absent.
Write ACTIVE_TASK.json with the bound task_id.
Write ACTIVE_RUNTIME.json with session_id, turn_id, and active_task_id.
Optionally seed task_goal when the user provided a stable goal.
Write SESSION_MEMORY.json for the session and TURN_AUDIT.json for the current turn.
rules: One active task pointer at a time.
Task directories are append-preserving; do not delete history when a task is paused or done.
Task slug normalization must be deterministic.
Manual bind-task is a repair/migration tool; the default runtime path is automatic session-stream binding.
If the user intent is ambiguous, do not silently overwrite another task.
